M35-T1.name: Performance defaults pass + clamp audit
M35-T1.deliverable: Evidence-based measurement pass across the 10 Phase 5 settings clamps (rag_chunk_size / rag_chunk_overlap / rag_similarity_threshold / agentic_max_steps / agentic_max_tokens / agentic_timeout_ms / planner_max_tickets / planner_max_depth / planner_escalation_threshold / copilot_interval_minutes) against local Ollama + llama3.1:8b. Measurement: cold 208563 ms / warm #1 66847 ms / warm #2 65872 ms; 2288-char prompt / 734 prompt_eval tokens / ~200 eval tokens; JSON response well-formed. Decision: all 10 defaults HELD (measurement justifies holding, not moving — 66s warm tick vs 120s agentic_timeout_ms = 0.55× utilisation; 5-min copilot cadence vs 66s tick = 4.5× headroom; 8000 token budget vs 1600 projected = 4× headroom). Full measurement block + per-clamp rationale table lives in apps/desktop/src/main/db/seed.ts header. Zero production behavior changes; zero new production files; clamp envelopes unchanged.

M35-T2.name: Cross-milestone integration E2E — RAG → NLU → agentic → planner → copilot
M35-T2.deliverable: NEW apps/desktop/e2e/phase-5-integration.spec.ts (495 lines). Single Playwright spec stitching the full Phase 5 stack (M28 → M29 → M30 → M31 → M32 → M33 → M34) in one session. Scenario: (1) M28/M29 chat round-trip via CEO with __ECHO_TEXT__:<marker> sentinel fires work.completed → RagIndexer embeds the reply → rag.stats embedding count grows above baseline (also asserts vault.upload still works end-to-end inside the same session). (2) M30 + M31 Cmd+K 'what is my team doing right now' → canned classifier routes complex_request → canned agentic provider scripts plan → query_employees → answer → answer card contains 'Team currently has' + system-agent thread persists ≥2 messages. (3) M32 Cmd+K 'decompose the frontend redesign into tickets' → amber write-side gate fires → Confirm dispatches second loop → decompose_project + delegate_subtask + answer; answer card contains 'Decomposed the frontend redesign'. (4) M33 copilot.configure manual tick → insightsGenerated ≥ 1. (5) M34 toolbar Sparkles toggle → sidebar mounts via data-copilot-sidebar-root → insight card via data-copilot-insight-id → dismiss X fires → card leaves DOM → copilot.dismissed event on append-only events table (invariant #11) → ask-input filled with __ECHO_AGENT__: sentinel → submit → sidebar closes → system-flagged thread count ≥1 (both system-agent from Step 2 AND system-copilot from Step 5). Regression guards inline: M30 destructive gate ABSENT across full session; M32 write-side gate fires ONLY in Step 3; Phase 5 badge verbatim. Launched with TEAM_X_RAG_TEST=1 + NODE_ENV=test. Zero production-code changes. Spec runtime 3.7s (under the ≤15s budget).

M35-T2.surpriseNote: During authoring, the first spec draft assumed the RagIndexer subscribed to vault bus events. Grep surfaced it only subscribes to work.completed + meeting.ended — vault files are retrieved through FTS5 at agent-turn time, not pre-indexed into embeddings. Corrected Step 1 to drive a chat round-trip via chat.send (preserving the vault.upload IPC call as an independent assertion that the Phase 4 vault write path still works inside the Phase 5 session). Single-iteration refactor; E2E passed on second run. Follow-up docblock-accuracy fix shipped 2026-04-19 as commit 54830fa after the post-T3 user correction surfaced that the same RagIndexer subscription misread had survived into the spec's header docblock text (two passages rewritten; spec body unchanged).

M35-T4.name: Phase 5 retrospective doc
M35-T4.deliverable: NEW docs/plans/2026-04-19-team-x-phase-5-retrospective.md (271 lines). Locked six-section structure per M35 plan doc §4.5 so future phase retros stay directly comparable: (1) What we shipped — one-liner per milestone M28 → M35 with commit range + unit/E2E delta + headline architectural seam; (2) What went well — 10 compounding patterns enumerated (canned test-seam quartet, {rows, truncated} envelope, data-* stable selector surface, pause-aware providerRouter wrapper, AbortController stop, is_system filter-sweep, atomic+ledger cadence, ABI rebuild dance, pure-helpers .ts + component .tsx split, invariant #11); (3) What cost us time — Vitest/Playwright ABI dance + sqlite-vec extension load path + FTS5 regression that birthed invariant #11 + malformed tool-call parser nudge-retry + F1/F2/F3/F4 follow-ups + @/ alias cascade + docblock accuracy drift + plan-vs-shipped test count drift; (4) What we deferred — insight export + multi-company aggregation + drag-to-reorder + capabilities frontmatter + post-launch telemetry digest + proactive autonomy + agent-to-agent negotiation; (5) Metrics — test growth table (612 → 1162 = +550 / +83%), E2E 4 → 11 specs / 12 cases, LOC delta (207 files / 43,919 insertions / 489 deletions), architectural surface delta (EventType 16 → 31, invariants 10 → 11, settings +10, migrations +5, pseudo-employees 0 → 2), follow-up count (4 closed), commit cadence adherence (80% strict, 100% ledger); (6) Phase 6 seeds — 7 prioritized hypotheses (post-release telemetry digest / cross-company rollups / proactive copilot → autonomous action / agent-to-agent negotiation / capabilities frontmatter / real customer demo / insight export), each with user value + architectural cost + risk scoped so any one could be a Phase 6 headline. Cross-link added from docs/plans/2026-04-13-team-x-phase-5-intelligence-layer.md §13 Decisions Log pointing at the retrospective for discoverability from the locked design authority. Zero code changes, zero tests, zero IPC/bus/migration work. Pure markdown deliverable per plan §3 T4 row.

M35-T3.name: Observability polish — Audit view chip coverage audit
M35-T3.deliverable: NEW apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/features/audit/audit-event-chip-helpers.ts (347 lines, pure helpers), NEW apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/features/audit/audit-event-chip.tsx (89 lines, component shell + re-exports), NEW apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/features/audit/audit-event-chip.test.tsx (263 lines, 28 unit tests), MODIFIED apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/features/audit/audit-view.tsx (-143 lines duplicated helpers removed, +10 lines chip import + component replacement). 10 newly-chipped Phase 5 event types: rag.index.indexed (blue), rag.index.reindexed (blue), rag.index.removed (gray), agent.step (sky), agentic.completed (emerald), agentic.failed (rose), copilot.analyzed (blue), copilot.insight (amber), copilot.dismissed (gray), copilot.expired (gray). Payload-aware row-summary formatters surface the most useful payload field per event (agent.step → kind + stepIndex + runId slice; copilot.analyzed → reason + insightsGenerated + merged + durationMs; rag.index.* → sourceKind + sourceId slice + chunkCount). Hard 140-char summary cap via clampSummary. Chip module split into pure-helpers `.ts` + component `.tsx` mirrors the existing step-card-narrow.ts + step-card.tsx convention — unblocks future renderer pure-helper extractions without vitest `@/` alias work. A11y contract: semantic color always paired with visible text label (WCAG 1.4.1); aria-label='Event type: <literal>' so screen readers announce wire-level type; data-event-type stable E2E selector surface (mirrors M31 data-step-kind + M34 data-copilot-insight-id). Three representative event types pinned per acceptance #3 (rag.index.indexed / agent.step / copilot.analyzed) across color class + display label + aria-label + summarizable predicate + row summary. M32 T6 planner regression guards (plan.proposed + task.delegated). Cross-cutting defaults (DEFAULT_COLOR fallback + title-cased unknowns + 140-char clamp invariant). rag.index.* chips land DEFENSIVELY — CLAUDE.md bus-event table documents them aspirationally but rag-indexer.ts:78,88 only handles work.completed + meeting.ended and writes directly via ragService.indexSource without a bus emit (verified via grep before chip-map authoring per plan carry-forward note).
M35-T3.testsDelta: +28 unit (1134 → 1162; plan estimated +3 — shipped richer coverage per no-cutting-corners mandate)

verificationGates.e2e: not re-run (renderer-only chip change; no E2E spec asserts on audit-view selectors; matches M35 T1's comment+test-only precedent — E2E baseline preserved at 12 cases / 11 spec files / 40.6s from M35 T2)

M35-T3.splitModulePattern: pure helpers in audit-event-chip-helpers.ts (no React, no Badge, no `@/` aliases — imports intentLabel via relative path `../command/intent-labels.js`) + thin component in audit-event-chip.tsx (imports Badge via `@/`, re-exports every helper). Test file imports ONLY from the helpers module so vitest resolves without configuring the `@/` alias — matches the existing step-card-narrow.ts + step-card.tsx precedent. audit-view.tsx imports `AuditEventChip` + `buildRowSummary` + `formatEventType` from the component module (re-exports make the split invisible to consumers).
M35-T3.a11yContract: 1. Semantic color ALWAYS paired with visible text label (WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color). 2. aria-label='Event type: <literal>' so screen readers announce wire-level type (e.g. 'Event type: copilot.analyzed'). Sighted users still see title-cased display label ('Copilot Analyzed'). 3. Focus ring inherited from Badge primitive's built-in `focus:ring-2 focus:ring-ring focus:ring-offset-2`. 4. data-event-type mirrors literal event type as stable E2E selector surface.
M35-T3.surpriseNote: First authoring pass placed all helpers + component in a single audit-event-chip.tsx file per the plan's filesTouched list. Vitest run surfaced two transitive `@/` alias resolution failures: (a) the test file's import of audit-event-chip.tsx cascaded into Badge which imports @/lib/utils (cn helper); (b) switching the chip's internal imports from @/ to relative did not help because Badge's own @/lib/utils import is a FILE-LEVEL dep that vitest still couldn't resolve. Fix was architectural rather than config-level: split the module into pure-helpers `.ts` (zero React, zero Badge, zero @/ aliases) + component `.tsx`, and point the test at the pure-helpers module directly. This mirrors the existing step-card-narrow.ts + step-card.tsx convention and unblocks future renderer unit tests without requiring a vitest @/ alias config change. Net new file: audit-event-chip-helpers.ts (one file over the plan's filesTouched list — acceptable because the split aligns with an ALREADY-ESTABLISHED codebase convention).
M35-T3.testCountDelta: Plan estimate was +3 unit tests pinning chip render for 3 representative event types. Shipped +28: each representative event type cluster (rag.index.indexed / agent.step / copilot.analyzed) decomposed into 5–8 granular assertions (color, display label, aria-label, summarizable predicate, well-formed summary, pluralization edge case, malformed-input guard) plus M32 T6 planner regression guards (plan.proposed + task.delegated) plus cross-cutting defaults (DEFAULT_COLOR fallback + title-cased unknowns + ROW_SUMMARY_MAX_CHARS clamp). Granular style is more diagnostic — each assertion has its own `it` name so failures pinpoint the exact regression.
